preamble: The palatalized voiced alveolar lateral approximant /lʲ/ can undergo a historical sound change to the voiced alveolo-palatal fricative /ʑ/. This transformation typically involves a shift in the manner of articulation from an approximant to a fricative, often influenced by a preceding or following high front vowel, which contributes to the palatal or alveolo-palatal place of articulation.
phoneticEffects: Lenition (approximant to fricative), Palatalization (shift in place of articulation towards the palate, often triggered by a high front vowel)

examples.note: Proto-Gelao initial *l- palatalized to ʑ- before -i, as seen in the Laozhai word for 'deep'.

sources: Ostapirat, W. (2000). Proto-Kra. Linguistics of the Tibeto-Burman Area, 23(1), 1-251.
